goal_title_suggestions: Follow a Real Program and Grow

variants: Download Hevy or Strong on your phone.
Open the App Store and install Hevy or Strong.
Pick one workout app — Hevy or Strong — and download it now.
tasks.why: You need a place to track your workouts before you start.

variants: Step on the scale and write down your weight.
Weigh yourself and note the number in your phone.
Check your weight today and save it somewhere you can find it.
tasks.why: Your starting weight is the only way to measure real progress.

variants: Take a front photo of yourself and save it.
Snap a quick photo of yourself today — front view — and keep it.
Take a starting photo right now and put it in a private album.
tasks.why: A before photo is the best way to see how far you've come.

variants: Create your free account in Hevy or Strong.
Open your new workout app and sign up.
Finish setting up your profile in your workout app.
tasks.why: A quick setup today means you're ready to log your first workout.

variants: Pick 3 days this week to work out and write them down.
Choose your 3 workout days — like Mon, Wed, Fri — and note them.
Decide which 3 days you'll work out this week.
tasks.why: Picking your days now means you won't skip when life gets busy.

variants: Set a reminder on your phone for your first workout day.
Add a phone alarm for your first workout this week.
Put a calendar alert on your first planned workout day.
tasks.why: A reminder makes it harder to forget.

variants: Do 10 pushups right now.
Get on the floor and do as many pushups as you can.
Do 10 pushups — drop to your knees if you need to.
tasks.why: Your first workout starts with one simple move.

variants: Do 10 squats — no weight needed.
Stand up and do 10 bodyweight squats.
Do 10 squats right where you are.
tasks.why: Squats work your whole lower body with zero equipment.

variants: Log what you just did in your workout app.
Open Hevy or Strong and add today's pushups and squats.
Write down your reps in your workout app — even if it's just a few.
tasks.why: Logging your first session builds the habit of tracking.

variants: Eat one extra chicken breast today.
Add a hard-boiled egg or Greek yogurt to your next meal.
Have a protein shake or some cottage cheese with your next meal.
tasks.why: More protein gives your muscles what they need to grow.

variants: Drink a full glass of water right now.
Fill a water bottle and drink it before your next meal.
Drink 2 glasses of water today — muscles need water to work.
tasks.why: Staying hydrated helps you feel better during workouts.

variants: Write down one food high in protein that you like.
Think of 2 protein foods you already eat and note them.
List 3 protein foods you enjoy — eggs, chicken, beans, etc.
tasks.why: Knowing your go-to protein foods makes eating better easy.

variants: Do 3 sets of 10 pushups with a 30-second rest between each.
Do 10 pushups, rest, do 10 more, rest, do 10 more.
Complete 3 rounds of pushups — 10 reps each, rest between rounds.
tasks.why: Doing multiple sets instead of one is how you build muscle faster.

variants: Do 3 sets of 10 squats.
Complete 3 rounds of 10 squats with a short rest.
Do 30 total squats in 3 sets of 10.
tasks.why: More sets means more work for your legs today.

variants: Log today's sets and reps in your workout app.
Open your app and write down 3x10 pushups and 3x10 squats.
Add your workout to your log right after you finish.
tasks.why: Tracking your sets shows you how much work you're really doing.

variants: Take a rest day — just go for a 5-minute walk.
Rest today, but get outside for a short walk.
Skip the workout today. Walk around the block once.
tasks.why: Your muscles grow while you rest, not just while you lift.

variants: Eat something with protein today — eggs, chicken, or beans.
Make sure you have protein in your next meal.
Add a protein food to your lunch or dinner today.
tasks.why: Rest days are when your body repairs — protein helps that happen.

variants: Look at your workout log and see what you did this week.
Open your app and review the workouts you logged.
Check how many days you worked out this week.
tasks.why: Seeing your progress keeps you motivated to keep going.

variants: Do 3 sets of 12 pushups today — 2 more reps than before.
Try 12 pushups per set instead of 10.
Bump up to 12 reps this set if you can.
tasks.why: Adding even 2 reps is how you get stronger over time.

variants: Do 3 sets of 12 squats.
Add 2 more reps to your squat sets today.
Go for 12 squats per set this time.
tasks.why: Small increases in reps add up to big gains over 90 days.

variants: Log your workout and compare to your first session.
Open your app and see how your numbers changed since day 3.
Check your log — are you doing more reps than day 1?
tasks.why: Seeing your improvement keeps you coming back.

variants: Look up 'how to do a good pushup' and watch a 1-min video.
Check your pushup form — search for a quick form tip online.
Watch a 60-second pushup form video on YouTube.
tasks.why: Good form means you build muscle faster and avoid getting hurt.

variants: Do 10 slow pushups focusing on your chest squeezing at the top.
Try 10 pushups, pausing for 1 second at the bottom.
Do 10 pushups as slowly and controlled as you can.
tasks.why: Slowing down makes the exercise harder and more effective.

variants: Log your form-focused pushups in your app.
Add today's 10 slow pushups to your workout log.
Write down what you focused on today in your log.
tasks.why: Tracking everything — even small sessions — builds the habit.

variants: Add a new move to your workout: do 10 lunges (5 each leg).
Try lunges today — 5 steps forward on each leg.
Do 10 lunges to work your legs in a new way.
tasks.why: Adding a new exercise keeps your body guessing and growing.

variants: Do your usual pushups and squats after the lunges.
Finish with 3x10 pushups and 3x10 squats.
After lunges, do 2 sets of 10 pushups and 10 squats.
tasks.why: Finishing your regular moves keeps your routine solid.

variants: Log all 3 exercises in your app today.
Write down lunges, pushups, and squats in your workout log.
Add the full workout to your tracker.
tasks.why: Tracking your full session shows your workout getting bigger.

variants: Eat an extra serving of protein at breakfast — eggs, yogurt, or a shake.
Add protein to your morning meal today.
Have a protein-heavy breakfast — 2 eggs or a cup of Greek yogurt.
tasks.why: Eating protein in the morning helps your body recover from yesterday.

variants: Do 20 pushups right after breakfast.
Drop and do pushups first thing in the morning.
Start your day with 20 pushups before checking your phone.
tasks.why: Morning movement gives you energy and gets the workout done early.

variants: Log today's pushups in your app.
Add your morning pushups to your workout tracker.
Record your reps before you forget.
tasks.why: Logging right after keeps your numbers accurate.

variants: Do 3 sets of 10 pushups, 10 squats, and 10 lunges — rest 30 sec between.
Run through pushups, squats, and lunges — 3 rounds of 10 each.
Complete your 3-exercise circuit: 3 rounds of 10 pushups, 10 squats, 10 lunges.
tasks.why: Doing your moves back-to-back saves time and burns more energy.

variants: Do a 30-second plank after the circuit.
Hold a plank for as long as you can — aim for 30 seconds.
Add a plank at the end — just get in position and hold it.
tasks.why: A strong core makes every other exercise easier.

variants: Log the full circuit — all 4 moves — in your app.
Write down every exercise you did today in your tracker.
Record your full workout before you sit down.
tasks.why: A full log today shows you how much your routine has grown.

variants: Rest day. Do a 5-minute stretch.
Take today off from working out and stretch your legs.
Rest today — sit on the floor and stretch for 5 minutes.
tasks.why: Stretching on rest days helps your muscles recover faster.

variants: Drink 6 glasses of water today.
Fill your water bottle twice today.
Track how much water you drink today — aim for 6 glasses.
tasks.why: Staying hydrated helps sore muscles feel better.

variants: Plan your next 3 workout days on your calendar.
Pick your workout days for next week right now.
Set 3 workout reminders for next week.
tasks.why: Planning ahead makes it much easier to follow through.

variants: Try to do 1 more rep than last time on every exercise.
Push for 13 pushups per set instead of 12.
Beat your last count by at least 1 rep on each move.
tasks.why: Doing 1 more rep than last time is exactly how muscles grow.

variants: Hold a plank for 10 seconds longer than yesterday.
Try to hold your plank for 40 seconds today.
Beat your plank time from last session by a few seconds.
tasks.why: Small improvements add up to a much stronger core.

variants: Log your new personal records in the app.
Write down your best reps for each exercise today.
Add your improved numbers to your tracker.
tasks.why: Personal records are proof you're getting stronger.

variants: Weigh yourself and compare to your day 1 number.
Step on the scale and check where you are vs. day 1.
Get your two-week weight check-in and note the number.
tasks.why: Checking in every 2 weeks shows if your plan is working.

variants: Take a front photo and compare it to your day 1 photo.
Snap a photo and put it side by side with your starting pic.
Take a 2-week progress photo from the same angle as day 1.
tasks.why: Photos often show changes the scale doesn't.

variants: Write one thing that got easier this week.
Think of one exercise that feels less hard than day 1.
Note one way your body already feels different.
tasks.why: Noticing progress — even small — keeps you going.

variants: Do 3 sets of pushups, squats, lunges, and a plank.
Run your full circuit: 3 rounds of all 4 moves.
Complete 3 sets of each exercise in your routine.
tasks.why: Week 3 starts — keep the habit going strong.

variants: Add a 5-min walk after your workout.
Walk around the block after your session.
Get outside for 5 minutes after you finish your workout.
tasks.why: Walking after a workout helps your body cool down and recover.

variants: Log the full workout.
Record every set in your tracker.
Add today's session to your workout app.
tasks.why: Consistent logging shows your streak building.

variants: Add one new protein food to your meals today.
Try a food you haven't eaten much — tuna, cottage cheese, or lentils.
Pick a new high-protein food and eat it today.
tasks.why: Mixing up protein sources keeps eating healthy from getting boring.

variants: Eat protein with every meal today.
Make sure each meal today has a protein food in it.
Add eggs, chicken, beans, or yogurt to every meal today.
tasks.why: Spreading protein across meals helps your muscles recover all day.

variants: Write down what you ate for protein today.
List the protein foods you had today.
Note your 3 protein meals in your phone.
tasks.why: Tracking what you eat makes it easier to hit your goals.

variants: Do 3 rounds of pushups, squats, and lunges — try to add 1 rep to each.
Workout day: all 3 moves, 3 sets, try 1 extra rep per set.
Hit your circuit and push for slightly more reps than last time.
tasks.why: Adding 1 rep at a time is the whole game of getting stronger.

variants: Hold your plank for 45 seconds.
See if you can hit 45 seconds on your plank today.
Plank for 40-50 seconds — as long as you can with good form.
tasks.why: Your plank time is a good sign of how much stronger you're getting.

variants: Log your workout.
Record today's reps and sets in your app.
Add the session to your tracker before you sit down.
tasks.why: Your log is proof you showed up today.

variants: Rest day. Stretch your chest and legs for 5 minutes.
Take today off from lifting and do a light stretch.
Rest today — spend a few minutes stretching your sore muscles.
tasks.why: Recovery days are built into any good routine.

variants: Drink plenty of water today.
Aim for 6 glasses of water on your rest day.
Stay hydrated today even though you're not working out.
tasks.why: Water helps your muscles repair on rest days.

variants: Read 1 tip about building muscle — something simple.
Search 'muscle building tip' and read one short article.
Watch a 60-second tip about working out — YouTube or Google.
tasks.why: Learning one new thing each week helps you improve faster.

variants: Do 4 rounds of your circuit today instead of 3.
Add one extra round to your workout today.
Go through pushups, squats, and lunges 4 times instead of 3.
tasks.why: More rounds is a simple way to make your workout harder.

variants: Rest only 20 seconds between sets.
Cut your rest time by 10 seconds today.
Try to keep moving with shorter breaks between sets.
tasks.why: Less rest makes your heart work harder and burns more.

variants: Log your 4-round workout.
Record that you completed 4 sets today in your app.
Write down your extra round in the tracker.
tasks.why: Logging the upgrade shows you pushed yourself.

variants: Cook a simple high-protein meal — eggs with anything, or chicken and rice.
Make a quick protein-heavy meal today — 2 eggs, some chicken, or tuna.
Prep a simple meal with protein as the main part.
tasks.why: Cooking your own meals makes it easy to control what you eat.

variants: Do 20 pushups as a midday break.
Get up and do 20 pushups in the middle of your day.
Take a pushup break — 20 reps before your next meal.
tasks.why: Quick movement during the day adds up and keeps your body active.

variants: Log your pushups from today.
Record your midday pushups in your app.
Add the 20 reps to your daily log.
tasks.why: Every set counts — even the ones between tasks.

variants: Do your full workout — circuit, plank, all of it.
Run the full circuit 3 times with your best effort.
Hit your workout today — same moves, push for your best.
tasks.why: Three weeks in — this is where habits start to stick for good.

variants: After your workout, eat something with protein within 30 minutes.
Have a protein snack right after your session.
Drink a shake or eat some chicken within 30 min of finishing.
tasks.why: Eating protein after a workout helps your muscles repair.

variants: Log the workout and your post-workout meal.
Record your session and what you ate after.
Add today's full workout and food to your tracker.
tasks.why: Tracking both food and workouts is the full picture.

variants: Rest today. Go for a 10-minute walk.
Take today easy — just walk around the block twice.
Active rest day: a slow 10-minute walk is perfect.
tasks.why: Walking on rest days keeps blood flowing to your muscles.

variants: Look at your workout log for the past 3 weeks.
Open your app and count how many workouts you've logged.
Review your last 21 days of sessions in your tracker.
tasks.why: Seeing 3 weeks of work is motivating — you earned this.

variants: Write down your 3-week win — one thing you're proud of.
Note one thing that's improved since day 1.
Write a quick sentence about how your body feels vs. 3 weeks ago.
tasks.why: Celebrating small wins helps you stay motivated.

variants: Try a new move: do 10 tricep dips using a chair.
Find a sturdy chair and do 10 dips — hands behind you, lower your body.
Add chair dips to your workout today — 10 reps.
tasks.why: Tricep dips add a new challenge that pushups don't hit.

variants: Finish with your pushups and squats as usual.
After dips, do 2 sets of 10 pushups and 10 squats.
Complete your regular moves after the new exercise.
tasks.why: Mixing new moves with your regular ones builds a fuller routine.

variants: Log the new exercise in your app.
Add chair dips to your tracker as a new exercise.
Record your first set of dips in your workout log.
tasks.why: Logging a new move is the start of tracking a new strength.

variants: Try a new protein snack today — cottage cheese, edamame, or jerky.
Pick up or make one new high-protein snack today.
Eat a protein snack you don't usually have — try something new.
tasks.why: Finding protein snacks you like makes it easy to eat enough.

variants: Do 15 pushups in one set without stopping.
Try 15 pushups straight through, no breaks.
See if you can do 15 pushups without resting.
tasks.why: Pushing to 15 in one go shows real progress.

variants: Log it.
Add your 15 pushups to your tracker.
Record your pushup set in the app.
tasks.why: A 15-rep set is worth recording.

variants: Do your full circuit: pushups, squats, lunges, dips, plank.
Run all 5 exercises today — 3 rounds of each.
Full workout day: 3 sets of all your moves.
tasks.why: Your routine is growing — this is a real workout now.

variants: Time your full workout — how long did it take?
Start a timer when you begin and stop it when you finish.
Check how many minutes your full session takes.
tasks.why: Knowing your workout time helps you plan your day.

variants: Log the workout and your total time.
Record all exercises and how long you worked out.
Write down your session with the total minutes in your tracker.
tasks.why: Tracking time shows how efficient your workouts are getting.

variants: Rest. Stretch your shoulders and back for 5 minutes.
Spend 5 minutes stretching your upper body — no workout today.
Rest day — sit on the floor and stretch your arms and back.
tasks.why: Shoulder and back stretches reduce soreness from pushups.

variants: Drink plenty of water and eat a protein-focused lunch.
Have a simple high-protein lunch — eggs, chicken, or tuna.
Make sure your lunch today has a good protein source.
tasks.why: Food and water on rest days fuel tomorrow's workout.

variants: Set your workout schedule for the next 7 days.
Plan your workout days for next week right now.
Pick 3 days next week and put them in your calendar.
tasks.why: Having a plan for next week means you won't skip days.

variants: Do your circuit and try to beat your reps from last session.
Push for 1 more rep on at least 2 exercises today.
Full workout — see if any numbers go up vs. last time.
tasks.why: Every workout you should try to do slightly more than last time.

variants: Hold your plank for 1 minute if you can.
Go for 60 seconds on your plank today.
Try for a full 1-minute plank hold.
tasks.why: A 1-minute plank is a real milestone — go for it.

variants: Log your workout and note your plank time.
Record your session and write down your plank seconds.
Add everything to your tracker — especially if you hit 60 seconds.
tasks.why: A 1-minute plank in your log is worth remembering.

variants: Weigh yourself and compare to day 14.
Step on the scale for your 4-week check-in.
Get your 28-day weight and write it next to your last numbers.
tasks.why: 4 weeks in is a great time to see how your body is changing.

variants: Take a progress photo from the same angle as your first two.
Snap a photo today and line it up with day 1 and day 14.
Take a 4-week photo from the same spot as your other check-ins.
tasks.why: Photos every 2 weeks give you a clear picture of your progress.

variants: Write your 4-week summary — what's better, what's harder.
Note 2 things that improved and 1 thing still challenging.
Quick review: what's easier now vs. day 1?
tasks.why: A short review helps you know what to focus on next.

variants: Full workout today — all your moves, 3 sets each.
Do your complete circuit with full effort.
Hit every exercise in your routine for 3 sets.
tasks.why: Month 1 is almost done — finish strong.

variants: After your workout, eat a protein-rich meal within 30 minutes.
Have a protein snack as soon as you finish your session.
Immediately after working out, eat something with protein.
tasks.why: Your muscles need protein right after a workout to rebuild.

variants: Log today's workout.
Add your session to your tracker.
Record all your sets and reps in the app.
tasks.why: One more session in the books.

variants: Do max pushups in one set — go until you can't anymore.
See how many pushups you can do without stopping.
Push out your max pushup reps — go to failure.
tasks.why: Your max reps after 30 days shows exactly how much stronger you are.

variants: Write down your max number and compare to day 1.
Note your pushup max and think about how many you could do on day 1.
Record your 30-day max pushups — compare to your starting number.
tasks.why: A bigger number than day 1 is real, measurable proof of progress.

variants: Celebrate making it 30 days — that's the hardest part.
Take a moment to appreciate 30 days of showing up.
You made it one month — write down one thing you're proud of.
tasks.why: 30 days of consistency is something worth celebrating.

variants: Month 2 starts. Do your full circuit — aim to beat your day 30 numbers.
Start month 2 strong — run your full circuit with fresh effort.
New month, same routine — go for 1 more rep on everything.
tasks.why: Month 2 is where real results start to show.

variants: Add a 2nd plank today — try 2 sets of planks.
Do your plank twice today for more core work.
Hold 2 planks — rest between — to build more core strength.
tasks.why: Doubling up on planks is a simple way to make your core work harder.

variants: Log your month 2 kickoff workout.
Record your day 31 session in your app.
Add today's sets to your tracker to start month 2 right.
tasks.why: Day 31 logged means month 2 has officially started.

variants: Look up 1 simple home workout move you haven't tried yet.
Search 'beginner home exercise' and find one new move.
Pick one new bodyweight exercise to add to your routine.
tasks.why: Adding variety keeps your body and mind engaged.

variants: Try the new move — do 10 reps.
Practice the new exercise you found — 10 reps to start.
Do the new move 10 times to see how it feels.
tasks.why: Trying it once is how you find out if you like it.

variants: Log the new exercise.
Add your new move to your workout tracker.
Record your first reps of the new exercise.
tasks.why: Tracking a new move means you can build on it next time.

variants: Do your full circuit — pushups, squats, lunges, dips, and your new move.
Run your whole routine including the new exercise you added.
Full workout today with all your exercises — 3 sets each.
tasks.why: Expanding your routine gives more muscles a reason to grow.

variants: Eat a protein-heavy meal today — more than usual.
Make protein the main event at every meal today.
Have eggs for breakfast and chicken or fish for lunch today.
tasks.why: More muscle work means your body needs more protein to recover.

variants: Log the full session.
Record every exercise in your tracker today.
Add all your sets and the new exercise to your app.
tasks.why: A complete log today shows how full your routine has become.

variants: Rest today. Do a light stretch for 5 minutes.
Take the day off — just stretch your legs and back.
Rest day — spend a few minutes loosening up your body.
tasks.why: Regular rest is what lets muscles actually grow.

variants: Eat your regular protein today even on a rest day.
Keep up protein intake today — eggs, chicken, or a shake.
Don't skip protein just because it's a rest day.
tasks.why: Your muscles still need fuel on rest days to repair.

variants: Look at your workout log and count your total sessions this month.
Count how many times you've worked out in the last 2 weeks.
Review your log and see your session count for month 2 so far.
tasks.why: Seeing your session count is motivating — every one counts.

variants: Do your workout with a focus on slow, controlled reps.
Slow each rep down — 2 seconds up, 2 seconds down.
Use slow reps today — no rushing, full control on every move.
tasks.why: Slowing down makes the same exercises harder and more effective.

variants: Hold the bottom of your squat for 2 seconds before coming up.
Pause at the lowest point of your squat — count to 2.
Add a 2-second pause at the bottom of each squat.
tasks.why: Pausing at the bottom makes squats much tougher without any extra weight.

variants: Log your slow-rep workout.
Record your session noting you used slow reps.
Add today's controlled session to your tracker.
tasks.why: Noting your form changes helps you know what worked.

variants: Try a new protein food this week — Greek yogurt, edamame, or tempeh.
Add one protein food you've never eaten much of.
Eat something new today that's high in protein.
tasks.why: Variety in protein keeps eating healthy from getting old.

variants: Do 25 pushups straight through in one set.
See if you can hit 25 pushups without stopping.
Push for 25 reps in one unbroken set of pushups.
tasks.why: 25 reps in one go is a strong milestone.

variants: Log your 25-rep set.
Record your 25 pushups in your tracker.
Add your max-rep set to your workout log.
tasks.why: 25 in one set is worth tracking.

variants: Full workout — all your moves, 3 sets, best effort.
Do your complete routine today.
Run every exercise in your plan — no skipping today.
tasks.why: Consistency is the most important thing in month 2.

variants: Try to reduce your rest time between sets to 20 seconds.
Rest only 20 seconds between each set today.
Short rest workout — only 20 seconds between sets.
tasks.why: Less rest increases intensity without adding more exercises.

variants: Log the workout.
Record your session in the app.
Add today's sets and reps to your tracker.
tasks.why: Your log is your proof of consistency.

variants: Rest. Get 8 hours of sleep tonight.
Take today off and prioritize sleep tonight.
Rest day — aim for 8 hours of sleep to help your muscles grow.
tasks.why: Most of your muscle growth actually happens while you sleep.

variants: Drink 8 glasses of water today.
Stay extra hydrated today — 8 full glasses.
Focus on water today — aim for 8 glasses.
tasks.why: Hydration on rest days speeds up recovery.

variants: Plan your workouts for the rest of the week.
Set 3 workout reminders for the next 5 days.
Pick your next 3 workout days and add them to your calendar.
tasks.why: Planning now removes the decision later.

variants: Do your full circuit — try for max reps on the last set of each exercise.
Last set of every exercise — push to your limit.
Full workout, and on your final set of each move, go until you can't.
tasks.why: Going to your limit on the last set is how you keep making gains.

variants: Do a 90-second plank if you can.
Try for 90 seconds on your plank hold today.
Push your plank to 90 seconds — break it into 2 holds if needed.
tasks.why: A 90-second plank is a strong core milestone.

variants: Log your max-effort sets.
Record your max reps for each exercise today.
Write down your hardest effort sets in your tracker.
tasks.why: Tracking max efforts shows your ceiling going up.

variants: Weigh yourself for a mid-month check-in.
Step on the scale today and note the number.
Get your day 40 weight and compare it to your earlier numbers.
tasks.why: Regular check-ins keep you honest about your progress.

variants: Take a progress photo.
Snap a side photo today and compare it to earlier ones.
Take a quick photo to track your month 2 progress.
tasks.why: A photo every few weeks shows changes you might not notice daily.

variants: Write one thing you want to improve in the next 2 weeks.
Set one small goal for the next 14 days.
Pick one thing to focus on between now and day 56.
tasks.why: Having a small focus goal keeps your training sharp.

variants: Do 4 sets of your circuit today — one more than usual.
Add an extra round to your workout today.
Go through all your exercises 4 times today.
tasks.why: More sets is one of the best ways to build more muscle.

variants: Eat protein at breakfast, lunch, and dinner today.
Make sure all 3 meals today have a protein source.
Add eggs, chicken, beans, or yogurt to every meal today.
tasks.why: Spreading protein across all your meals helps your body use it better.

variants: Log your 4-set workout.
Record that you did 4 rounds in your tracker.
Add your extra-round session to the app.
tasks.why: A 4-set day in your log shows you're leveling up.

variants: Rest. Do a quick full-body stretch.
Take today off and spend 5 minutes stretching.
Rest day — loosen up your whole body with a light stretch.
tasks.why: Stretching after hard workout days speeds up how fast you recover.

variants: Review what you've done in the past 6 weeks.
Open your log and count all your sessions since day 1.
Look at 6 weeks of logged workouts and see how far you've come.
tasks.why: Seeing 6 weeks of work is a big motivator to keep going.

variants: Write down what you want month 2 to finish with.
Set a goal for the end of month 2 — specific reps or a new move.
Write one thing you want to achieve by day 60.
tasks.why: A clear target for the next 2 weeks keeps your training focused.

variants: Full workout — focus on your weakest exercise and give it extra effort.
Pick the hardest exercise in your routine and do 1 extra set of it.
Do your whole circuit, then add an extra set of the move you find hardest.
tasks.why: Your weakest move is the best one to focus on — that's where growth hides.

variants: Do your plank and try to beat your best time.
Hold your plank and go until you can't — beat your last record.
Push your plank time as long as you can today.
tasks.why: Your personal best is always worth chasing.

variants: Log your workout with special note on your weak-point exercise.
Record all sets and note which exercise you focused on.
Add today's session and mark the extra set.
tasks.why: Noting your focus areas helps you track what you improved.

variants: Try one new food this week that has lots of protein.
Pick a protein food you've never cooked — lentils, tempeh, or edamame.
Cook or eat something new that's high in protein today.
tasks.why: Variety keeps your diet sustainable for the long run.

variants: Do 30 pushups in one set.
See if you can hit 30 pushups without stopping.
Go for 30 unbroken pushups — push to 30.
tasks.why: 30 pushups in one go is a real strength milestone.

variants: Log your 30-rep pushup set.

tasks.why: Your day 1 and day 90 photos are the proof of 90 days of work.

variants: Weigh yourself and write your final number next to day 1.
Step on the scale for the last time and record your weight.
Get your final weight and compare the full journey.
tasks.why: Your final weight next to your starting weight tells the whole story.

variants: Write down what you accomplished in 90 days.
List 3 things you can do now that you couldn't do on day 1.
Celebrate finishing — write one sentence about what this meant to you.
tasks.why: You showed up for 90 days. That deserves to be recognized.
